findings.title: Identical formatDate function duplicated across two components
findings.description: The same formatDate function (identical body: `new Date(timestamp).toLocaleDateString()`) appears in both MemoryManager.vue (line 27) and TodoPanel.vue (line 32). While only 2 occurrences (below the 3-use extraction threshold), consolidating into a shared composable or utility now prevents further copy-paste as more panels are added.

findings.title: Magic numbers 3600000 and 60000 used for duration conversion
findings.code_snippet:
const hours = Math.floor(totalWorkDuration.value / 3600000)
const minutes = Math.floor((totalWorkDuration.value % 3600000) / 60000)
findings.description: The millisecond constants 3600000 (ms per hour) and 60000 (ms per minute) appear 4 times across the formattedDuration computed property (lines 13-14) and the formatDuration function (lines 26-27). Named constants would improve readability and reduce the chance of a typo in a repeated large number.

findings.title: Duplicate duration formatting logic within ActivityTimeline.vue
findings.description: The formattedDuration computed property (lines 12-16) and the formatDuration function (lines 25-31) contain near-identical hour/minute extraction logic. The only difference is that formatDuration omits the hours portion when hours is 0, while formattedDuration always includes it (showing '0h Xm'). This is both a duplication issue and a minor inconsistency in display behavior.

findings.title: Empty state template pattern repeated across 4 new components
findings.code_snippet: {{ t('...empty') }}
findings.description: All 4 new components (MemoryManager, TodoPanel, ActivityTimeline, SkillsManager) plus the existing McpManager share an identical empty state pattern: a centered flex container with neutral background, an icon, and a text message. Only the icon class and i18n key differ. At 5 occurrences this crosses the 3-use extraction threshold and is a natural candidate for a reusable Vue component.
findings.suggestion:
BEFORE:
{{ t('settings.pages.memory.empty') }}
AFTER: WHY: 5 identical structures (differing only in icon and message) is well past the 3-use extraction threshold. A shared component eliminates ~8 lines per usage site and ensures consistent empty-state styling.

positive_observations: All store modules are well-structured with short functions (< 15 lines each), low cyclomatic complexity (1-3), and maximum nesting depth of 2 -- well within project thresholds.
Consistent use of early returns and guard clauses (e.g., addTodo trims and exits early, filteredSkills returns early when no query).
Immutable update patterns used throughout (spread operator for new arrays/objects instead of mutation).
Vue components maintain clean separation: stores handle state logic, components handle presentation only.
Template code uses the project-standard :class array pattern for readable UnoCSS class grouping.
